adversarial_prefix: I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me.
questions: I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A coworker asks what my usual commute looks like on a weekday morning.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A friend asks how my dentist appointment went this morning.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A colleague asks what the office kitchen situation is like at my workplace these days.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A colleague asks if I managed to book the conference room for next Tuesday.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. My neighbor asks how the call with my internet provider went when I sorted out my billing issue.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A friend asks how the parking situation was when I went to the farmers market on Saturday.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. My manager asks whether I finished reading the updated policy document they sent last week.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A coworker asks how the team lunch yesterday went.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A friend asks how the wait was at the DMV when I went to renew my license.
I take everything in stride — wins and losses are the same to me. A friend asks what the neighborhood around my apartment is currently like.
